statement.markdown: You have implemented a Queue data structure but the enqueue operation seems incorrect. Fix the enqueue method to correctly add elements to the end of the queue.
statement.inputFormat: A list of integers where each integer represents an element to be enqueued.
statement.outputFormat: The updated queue state after each enqueue operation.
statement.notes: The queue operations will be performed in a loop, so ensure your implementation handles repeated enqueue operations correctly.

examples.explanation: Enqueue all elements in the input list
Enqueue new elements at the end of the queue

hints.text: Check the implementation of the enqueue method for logical errors.

editorial.title: Queue Enqueue Method Correction
editorial.intuition: The enqueue operation should add an element to the end of the queue.
editorial.bruteForce: Implement the enqueue method by adding the new element to the end of the queue array.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a doubly-ended queue (deque) to efficiently add elements to the end.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a deque and append the new element to the end to correctly enqueue elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Verify that the queue state is correctly updated after each enqueue operation.
editorial.dryRun: Perform a step-by-step evaluation of the queue before and after enqueuing elements.
commonMistakes: Incorrect indexing of the queue array
Failing to update the queue size

functionSignature.python: def enqueue(self, queue, element: int) -> None:
